{{Quote|Is it battle that stirs you, to meet an enemy blade upon blade? Such is the way of the greatest of the Jedi warriors…the Weapon Masters.|[[Kreia]]|<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref>}}
A '''Jedi Weapon Master''' was a title given to those [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] who pursued the study of physical combat with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], especially of exotic weapons (or unusual styles) under the distinct schooling division known as the [[Jedi Guardian/Legends|Jedi Guardian]].

==Overview==
Some of the greatest fighters of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], they had existed for many centuries though were few in number. While the title had been formally distributed before, it wasn't until Guardian [[Jedi ace]] [[Crix Sunburris]] described the qualification of becoming a specialist in his contribution to the text of ''[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force]]''.<ref name="TJP" />

Setting aside their lightsabers, Weapon Masters would spend years of study, honing their skills in the art of their chosen weapon. Becoming highly skilled in their art, they were well respected within the Order and without. Mastering the use of either a [[Double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|double-bladed lightsaber]], [[Lightsaber pike/Legends|lightsaber pike]], [[Quarterstaff/Legends|quarterstaff]], [[San-Ni staff]], whips, or flails, was all that needed to be done to be considered a Weapon Master by the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]]. Additionally, ranged weapons like blasters and the use of one's body as a weapon were sufficient to receive this recognition.<ref name="TJP" />

From the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]] to the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], Weapons Masters appeared more frequently, for it was a long period of conflict and violence. However, after the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan|supposed destruction]] of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], they became less common within the Order. None were known to have survived the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]].<ref name="PotJ">''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]''</ref>

Jedi Masters [[Thon]] and [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]] were both recognized Weapon Masters of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] era.<ref name="PotJ" /> Following the [[Ruusan Reformations/Legends|Ruusan Reformation]], one of the most noted Weapon Masters was the famed [[Raskta Lsu]].<ref name="Darth Bane: Rule of Two">''[[Darth Bane: Rule of Two]]''</ref> Later, Master [[Waldan Bridger]] was considered especially skilled with the ancient San-Ni staff.<ref name="The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> Before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], [[Sora Bulq/Legends|Sora Bulq]],<ref name="GatORW">''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''</ref> [[Anoon Bondara]] and [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] were among the most renowned Weapon Masters of their generation.<ref name="PotJ" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:JediWeaponMaster.jpg|thumb|right|150px|A [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] Weapons Specialist, wielding twin [[Sword/Legends|swords]].]]
''Jedi Weapon Master'' first appeared as a prestige class for the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' in ''[[Star Wars Gamer #9]]'' and was featured in the ''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]''.

It also appeared in the video game ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', where it is one of the six prestige classes open to a level 15 character, and one of the three light side choices. In the game, the class corresponds to the class [[Sith Marauder]], as both focus on their own strength with a blade, and are more fit for heavy combat than the other prestige classes available: the stealth focused [[Sith assassin/Legends|Sith assassin]], similar to [[Jedi Watchman/Legends|Jedi Watchman]] - and [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]], akin to [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] that is supposed to increase the player's skill with the Force.

In the ''[[Star Wars Miniatures]]'' set [[Champions of the Force (Star Wars Miniatures)|''Champions of the Force'']], the Jedi Weapon Master character resembles [[Cin Drallig/Legends|Cin Drallig]].
